questions.question: What does strict TypeScript configuration help with?
options: Catching more mistakes at compile time
questions.explanation: Strict flags increase the amount of feedback TypeScript gives you before code ships.

questions.question: What is a path alias useful for?
questions.explanation: Path aliases like @/components/Button keep imports clean and easier to move later.

questions.question: What does noImplicitAny help prevent?
options: Untyped values sneaking into the codebase silently
questions.explanation: noImplicitAny forces you to be explicit instead of letting values become any by accident.

questions.question: Why keep tsconfig aligned across a team?
options: So everyone gets the same compiler behavior
So Git hooks can be skipped
questions.explanation: Shared compiler settings help the team avoid surprise differences between machines.
